channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: GOP looks to avoid disaster in Nebraska Senate race
item.description: Republicans are looking to avoid a disaster in the Nebraska Senate race amid signs that an independent candidate could be giving Sen. Deb Fischer (R-Neb.) a run for her money.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands join pro-Palestinian rallies around the globe as Oct. 7 anniversary nears
item.description: Police used tear gas and water cannons to disperse violent demonstrators in Rome as tens of thousands pro-Palestinian protesters took to the streets in European cities and around the globe, calling for a cease-fire as the first anniversary of the Hamas attacks on Israel approached.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Top US trade official sees progress in helping workers. Voters will decide if her approach continues
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the U.S. trade representative, Katherine Tai is legally required to avoid discussing the presidential election. But her ideas about fair trade are on the ballot in November.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: KJP slammed after Hurricane Helene over mixed messages on whether FEMA resources used for migrants
item.description: White House press secretary Karine Jean-Pierre is under fire for apparently contradicting herself regarding the use of FEMA funds for illegal immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As affordable housing disappears, states scramble to shore up the losses
item.description: While Americans continue to struggle under high rents, as many as 223,000 affordable housing units could disappear in the next five years.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedyan And Arushi Gupta, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FEMA has faced criticism and praise during Helene. Here's what it does — and doesn't do
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Federal Emergency Management Agency is tasked with helping states and communities hit by disasters like Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Michigan, Harris doesn't get hoped-for firefighters endorsement amid shifting labor loyalties
item.description: REDFORD TOWNSHIP, Mich. (AP) — It was the perfect place to welcome the endorsement of the firefighters union — a gleaming new firehouse in a blue-collar town just outside of Detroit in the key battleground state of Michigan.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Civil rights groups ask to extend voter registration deadlines in hurricane-ravaged states
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With registration deadlines looming, Democrats and civil rights groups are asking election officials in the states ravaged by Hurricane Helene to give voters more time. A judge in South Carolina on Friday extended that state's deadline to Oct.
item.dc:creator: AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris, Trump campaigns send surrogates to Las Vegas to court middle class, Latinos
item.description: With a month until Election Day, Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Donald Trump sent their surrogates to Las Vegas this week to campaign for Nevada’s six electoral votes.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: County official accuses two supervisors of preventing his promotion in filed claim
item.description: A former Registrar of Voters filed a claim against the county of San Diego, specifically making allegations against two sitting members on the Board of Supervisors.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in key swing states, starting with Pennsylvania
item.description: Obama’s first campaign stop for Harris will be in the Pittsburgh area on Oct. 10, according to a senior campaign official.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says he doesn't know whether Israel is holding up peace deal to influence 2024 US election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden had terse words for Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Friday, and said he didn't know whether the Israeli leader was holding up a Mideast peace deal in order to influence the outcome of the 2024 U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics: Harris' Heavy Hitter
item.description: The latest updates from the 2024 campaign trail, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former NIH official accused of making emails 'disappear' pleads Fifth to COVID subcommittee
item.description: A National Institutes of Health employee who served the agency for over three decades is pleading the Fifth after the House COVID Subcommittee demanded her testimony on allegedly making documents "disappear" for coworkers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court declines to block Biden limits on methane, toxic pollution
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday declined to block Biden administration limits on planet-warming methane from oil and gas production and toxic pollution from coal plants.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: New Yorkers think Eric Adams should resign after corruption indictment: poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Nearly 70% of New York City residents think Mayor Eric Adams should resign following his corruption indictment, according to a Marist College poll released Friday. Another 63% of New Yorkers think Gov. Kathy Hochul should remove Adams from his position if he does not resign.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Gloria, Turner compete in San Diego mayoral race debate
item.description: The debate between San Diego Mayor Todd Gloria and police officer and challenger Larry Turner covered a wide range of issues from transportation, flood infrastructure failures, housing shortages and of course homelessness.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: Pressure building on Mayor Eric Adams following federal indictment
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Pressure continues to build on Mayor Eric Adams -- with more federal corruption charges possible. On Thursday, the mayor seems to have forced out his schools chancellor sooner than expected, with the governor telling him to make sure he keeps the city running smoothly.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Debate performance gives Vance 2028 White House boost
item.description: Sen. JD Vance’s (R-Ohio) debate performance is giving him a shot in the arm to become a leading 2028 presidential contender no matter what happens in November between former President Trump and Vice President Harris.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: In excerpt from new memoir, Melania Trump says women have the ‘right to choose’ abortion
item.description: The former first lady says she is a longtime supporter of abortion rights. Her memoir is coming out a year after former President Donald Trump said he was "able to kill Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: Are Nevada voters swayed by promises of 'no tax on tips'?
item.description: Reporter John Langeler from KLAS in Las Vegas joins "The Hill" to discuss whether Nevada voters are swayed by proposals to eliminate federal taxes on tips.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Ranked choice voting is on the ballot in several states
item.description: Ranked choice voting could be coming to states like Colorado, Nevada and Oregon. Here's what to know.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors lay out new evidence in Trump election case, accuse him of having 'resorted to crimes'
item.description: A court filing unsealed Wednesday argues that the former president is not entitled to immunity from prosecution over his failed bid to remain in power.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Vance called Harris a 'border czar' during the VP debate. What does that mean?
item.description: Republicans, including former President Donald Trump and his 2024 running mate, Senator J.D. Vance of Ohio, have increasingly referred to Vice President Kamala Harris as a failed "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: Harris and Trump change their campaign plans after Hurricane Helene's destruction
item.description: Hurricane Helene is scrambling the presidential candidates' plans this week. Democratic nominee Kamala Harris is cutting short a campaign visit to Las Vegas to return to Washington for briefings. Republican candidate Donald Trump is heading to Georgia to see the storm's impact.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, JILL COLVIN and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden administration expands asylum restrictions at southern border
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it will expand its asylum restrictions at the U.S.-Mexico border, cementing tougher standards that will cut off access to protections for many migrants.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
